task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium location to Tromsø and time to Dec 21, 2023, 11:00 UTC
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium Location window showing Tromsø (approx 69.64° N, 18.95° E, 10m) and the Date/Time window showing 2023-12-21 11:00:00. Alternatively, check the bottom status bar for these values.

task_completion.description: Configure required display flags in Stellarium
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium sky view showing an azimuthal grid (circular grid lines), constellation lines and labels, cardinal direction points (e.g., S, N), no ground/landscape (stars and grid continue below the horizon line), and atmosphere enabled (sky might have a twilight glow). Bottom toolbar icons for these features should be toggled appropriately.

task_completion.description: Capture centered screenshots of Venus and Polaris
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots where Stellarium's view is centered on Venus and Polaris (target crosshairs or labels may be visible). There should be evidence of capturing the screenshots (e.g., Stellarium's on-screen 'Screenshot saved' message, a screenshot tool, or the files visible in a file manager at /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/).

task_completion.description: Capture a wide-field screenshot of the southern horizon
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing a wide-field view of the sky in Stellarium, oriented towards the South (the 'S' cardinal point should be visible near the bottom center).

task_completion.description: Create the production lighting reference document
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor open with the file named 'polar_night_lighting_notes.txt' (saved on the Desktop). The text must include the location (Tromsø), the date (Dec 21, 2023), notes about polar night/twilight conditions, and mentions of Venus and Polaris.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to change settings and view the sky.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for open Stellarium configuration windows, toolbar interactions, and active panning/selecting in the sky view, rather than just editing configuration files via terminal.

integrity.description: The notes file was created and edited properly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like Mousepad, gedit, or nano) being used to write the notes, rather than just echoing text into a file without verifying its contents.

integrity.description: The sky views appear to be genuinely rendered by Stellarium.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should clearly be from the Stellarium application interface, not images downloaded from a web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: Tromsø (Latitude 69.6492° N, Longitude 18.9553° E, Altitude 10m). Target Date/Time: 2023-12-21 11:00:00 UTC. Required Display Settings: Atmosphere ON, Landscape OFF, Constellation drawing ON, Constellation names ON, Azimuthal grid ON, Cardinal points ON. Expected Outputs: At least 3 screenshots in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/ and a text file at /home/ga/Desktop/polar_night_lighting_notes.txt containing location, date, twilight notes, and mentions of Venus and Polaris.
